t: Adding Recurring Leaderboards to Your Game
a: Encourage competition in your games by adding leaderboards that have a duration and repeat.

t: Adding an access point to your game
a: Provide your users a convenient connection to the Game Center dashboard.

t: Adding voice chat to multiplayer games
a: Enable players to voice chat with all, or groups of, players in a multiplayer game.

t: Assigning players to teams using rules
a: Set criteria for assigning players to teams in your game using matchmaking rules.
Confirm player credentials and device capabilities and check for account restrictions.

t: Choosing a leaderboard for your challenges
a: Understand what gameplay works well when configuring challenges in your game.

t: Connecting players with their friends in your game
a: Give players the ability to connect and interact with friends in your game.
Use activities to surface game content to players and encourage them to connect with each other.
Encourage friendly competition by adding challenges to your game.

t: Creating matchmaking rules for backward compatibility
a: Add matchmaking rules that support previous classic matchmaking versions of your game.
Develop games where multiple players interact in real time.
Create a leaderboard for your game that ranks player scores based on a schedule.
Develop games where multiple players take turns and can exchange data while waiting for their turn.
Review unsupported symbols and their replacements.
Review unsupported symbols and their replacements.
Review unsupported symbols and their replacements.
Review unsupported symbols and their replacements.
Review unsupported symbols and their replacements.
Provide an interface for players to navigate to their Game Center data from your game.

t: Encourage progress and competition with leaderboards
a: Let players measure their own progress and compare their skills with friends and others.
The amount of time that passes before an exchange times out.

t: Exchanging data between players in real-time games
a: Send data between players in a real-time multiplayer game.

t: Exchanging data between players in turn-based games
a: Add the ability for players to exchange game data and send messages while waiting for their turns.

t: Finding multiple players for a game
a: Discover and invite other players to participate in a real-time game.

t: Finding players for custom server-based games
a: Connect players to your custom server-hosted games by creating game sessions with hosted matches.
Create a more customized and optimized gaming experience by using matchmaking rules to find players in a real-time game.

t: Finding players with similar skill levels
a: Add a rule to find players in a range of skill levels to balance competitive gameplay.
An object that allows players to view and manage their Game Center information from within your game.
Specifies the corner of the screen to display the access point.
The lower-left corner of the screen.
The lower-right corner of the screen.
The upper-left corner of the screen.
The upper-right corner of the screen.
An achievement you can award a player as they make progress toward and reach a goal in your game.
A type of challenge where a player must earn another player’s achievement.
An object containing the text and artwork used to present an achievement to a player.

An `GKAchievementViewController` object provides a standard user interface to display achievement progress for the local player. If the [GKGameCenterViewController](/documentation/gamekit/gkgamecenterviewcontroller) class is available, you should use it instead.

An object implementing the [GKAchievementViewControllerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkachievementviewcontrollerdelegate) protocol is called when the user dismisses the achievements view controller. Typically, this protocol is implemented by the object in your game that originally displayed the achievements user interface.
A class that provides common data and methods for the different player objects.
A challenge issued by the local player to another player.
A completion block that provides information about the player who issues a challenge and the players who receive it.
A completion block that provides information about the player who issues a challenge and the players who receive it.
An object that represents the static metadata you define for the challenge.
The `GKChallengeEventHandler` class is used to respond to events related to challenges sent or received by the local player.
You implement the [GKChallengeEventHandlerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkchallengeeventhandlerdelegate) delegate to control how challenges are displayed in your game.
An object that responds to challenge events.
The state of a challenge.
The player successfully completed the challenge.
The player declined the challenge.
The challenge isn’t valid because an error occurred.
The player issued a challenge, but the other player hasn’t accepted or refused it.
The object representing the currently signed-in iCloud user.
Possible connection states for a player
The player is connected to the game session.
The player is not connected to the game session.
An object that provides the ability to present the dashboard in macOS games.
The error structure used by this framework.

The error domain for general game errors.
A one-day exchange request timeout.
An exchange request timeout that doesn’t expire.
Your game uses the `GKFriendRequestComposeViewController` class to present a screen that allows the local player to send friend requests to other players.

The [GKFriendRequestComposeViewControllerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkfriendrequestcomposeviewcontrollerdelegate) protocol is implemented by delegates of the [GKFriendRequestComposeViewController](/documentation/gamekit/gkfriendrequestcomposeviewcontroller) class. The delegate is called when the player dismisses the friend request.
Constants that indicate if the local player grants access to their friends list.
The player authorized your game to access their list of friends.
Access to the player’s friends’ data denied.
The player hasn’t choosen whether your game may access their friends list.
Access to the player’s list of friends restricted.

The game activity is active.
The game activity has ended. This is a terminal state.
The game activity is initialized but has not started.
The game activity is paused.

An object that responds to activity events.
Play Style of the game activity. It can be either Asynchronous or Synchronous.
The delegate that GameKit calls when the player dismisses the dashboard.
The dashboard that allows players to access their Game Center data in your game.
The type of content for the view controller to present.
The view controller should present a list of achievements.
The view controller should present a list of challenges.
The view controller should present the dashboard.

The view controller should present leaderboard sets or leaderboards if there are no sets.
The view controller should present the friends list.
The view controller should present the local player’s profile.

A user interface you can use to invite other users into a tvOS game session.
A protocol you implement to respond to changes to a sharing user interface.
An invitation to join a match sent to the local player from another player.
A protocol that handles invite events from Game Center.
A player’s response to an invitation to join a match.
A response when the player accepts the invitation.
A response when the player rejects the invitation.
A response when the system fails to deliver the invitation to the player.

Possible responses from an invitation to a remote player.
A leaderboard for a game that Game Center stores.
Information about a single score by a player on a leaderboard.
Specifies whether a leaderboard is recurring.
A leaderboard that never expires, showing all-time rankings of all players.
A leaderboard that recurs, allowing players a fresh start to compete and earn higher ranks in each ocurrence.
Specifies the type of players for filtering data.
Loads only data for friends of the local player.

The `GKLeaderboardViewController` class provides a standard user interface that displays leaderboard scores to the player. If the [GKGameCenterViewController](/documentation/gamekit/gkgamecenterviewcontroller) class is available, you should use it instead.

The [GKLeaderboardViewControllerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkleaderboardviewcontrollerdelegate) protocol is implemented by delegates of the [GKLeaderboardViewController](/documentation/gamekit/gkleaderboardviewcontroller) class. The delegate is called when the player dismisses the leaderboard.
The local player who signs in to Game Center on the device running the game.
A protocol that handles events for Game Center players.
A peer-to-peer network between a group of players that sign into Game Center.
The mechanism used to transmit data to other players.
Sends data continuously until the recipients successfully receive it or the connection times out.
Sends data once even if an error occurs.
An object that receives connection status and data transmitted in a multiplayer game.
A type for game-specific properties that Game Center uses to find players when using matchmaking rules.
An object that encapsulates the parameters to create a real-time or turn-based match.
The kind of match managed by Game Center.
A match hosted on your private server.
A peer-to-peer match hosted by Game Center.
A turn-based match hosted by Game Center.
An object that represents matchmaking results, including the players that join the match and their properties that matchmaking rules uses.
An object that creates matches with other players without presenting an interface to the players.
An interface that allows a player to invite other players to a real-time game and automatch to fill any empty slots.
An object that handles when the status of matchmaking changes.
Possible modes that a multiplayer game uses to find matches.
A mode that matches the local player only with players who are also actively looking for a match.
The default matchmaking mode.
A mode that matches the local player only with players who they invite, and doesn’t use automatch to fill empty slots.
A mode that matches the local player only with nearby players.
A Game Center-style banner that displays a message to the local player.
The state of a peer known to the session.
A peer not connected to the session, but one that the session can connect to.
A peer connected to the session.
A peer attempting to connect to the session.
A peer that disconnected from the session.
A peer that is no longer interested in receiving connections.
Network connections available to the peer picker dialog.
A Bluetooth connection to a device.
An Internet-based connection.
Provides a standard user interface to allow one iOS device to discover and connect to another.

The [GKPeerPickerControllerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkpeerpickercontrollerdelegate) protocol is implemented on an object to customize the behavior of a [GKPeerPickerController](/documentation/gamekit/gkpeerpickercontroller) object. The delegate is called by the peer picker to create a session object and to respond as the session is configured by the controller.
A remote player who the local player running your game can invite and communicate with through Game Center.
The size of a photo that Game Center loads.
Loads a normal-sized photo.
Loads a small photo.
A notification that posts after GameKit initializes the local player.
The possible states of a connection to a match.
A state in which a player connects to the match and can receive data.
A state in which a player disconnects from the match and can’t receive data.
An undetermined state in which the player can’t receive data.
A notification that posts when a player object’s data changes.
A constant for a player ID that’s no longer available.
Describes the release state of an App Store Connect resource, such as an Achievement or Leaderboard.
The system can’t determine the release state of the resource.
An object that represents a file containing saved game data.
A protocol that handles events related to saving game data.
An object containing information for a score that was earned by the player.
A type of challenge where a player must beat the leaderboard score of another player.
The mechanism used to transmit data to other peers.
The data is sent continuously until it is successfully received by the intended recipients or the connection times out.
The data is sent once and is not sent again if a transmission error occurred.
A [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession) object provides the ability to discover and connect to nearby iOS devices using Bluetooth or Wi-fi.

An object implements the [GKSessionDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gksessiondelegate) protocol to control the behavior of a [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession) object. The delegate is called when other visible peers change their state relative to the session. It is also called to determine whether another peer is allowed to connect to the session.
Error codes for the session error domain.
A peer that invited the session to connect to them canceled the connection request.
Bluetooth is not currently available.
The connection to another peer closed unexpectedly.
The attempt to establish a connection with another peer failed.
An error occurred in the [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession) object’s connection code.
The data your application attempted to send was too large for the session to transmit in a single call.
The peer your application tried to connect to refused the connection.
The peer your application attempted to connect to has already requested a connection to your session.
A serious error occurred inside [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession).
A parameter had an unexpected value.
Reserved for future use.
A peer with the specified `peerID` string could not be found.
An error occurred outside of the [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession) object’s control, such as memory allocation.
The operation could not be completed in the specified timeout period.
An error occurred in the [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession) object’s transport code.
Reserved for when the error does not fit in another category above.
An error occurred in [GKSession](/documentation/gamekit/gksession).
Modes that determine how a session interacts with other peers.
A client searches for servers advertising the same [sessionID](/documentation/gamekit/gksession/sessionid) property.
A peer advertises like a server and searches like a client.
A server advertises itself to local devices using its [sessionID](/documentation/gamekit/gksession/sessionid) property.
The mechanism used to send messages to other players in a game session.
The data is sent continuously until it is successfully received by the intended recipients or the connection times out.
The data is sent once and is not sent again if a transmission error occurs.

The [GKTurnBasedEventHandler](/documentation/gamekit/gkturnbasedeventhandler) class is used to respond to important messages related to turn-based matches. To use it, call the [shared()](/documentation/gamekit/gkturnbasedeventhandler/shared()) class method to get the singleton instance and assign an object that implements the [GKTurnBasedEventHandlerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkturnbasedeventhandlerdelegate) protocol to its [delegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkturnbasedeventhandler/delegate) property. All methods are called on the main thread.

The [GKTurnBasedEventHandlerDelegate](/documentation/gamekit/gkturnbasedeventhandlerdelegate) protocol is implemented by an object to receive notifications events for turn-based matches. All methods are called on the main thread.
The protocol that handles turn-based and data-exchange events between participants in a match.
Exchange request information that participants send in a turn-based match.
Details about a recipient’s response to an exchange request.
The status of an exchange or reply.
GameKit sent the exchange request to recipients but not all recipients replied.
The sender canceled the exchange request.
All recipients of the exchange request replied.
The current participant saved the exchange request.
The state of the exchange request is unknown.
An object that encapsulates the match data for games where players take turns.

The participant reaches a game-specific outcome.
The participant finishes in first place.
The participant finishes in fourth place.
The participant loses the match.

The participant finishes in second place.
The participant finishes in third place.
The participant ties the match.
The match ends because the time limit expires.
The participant wins the match.

A match that is in an unknown state.
An interface that allows a player to invite other players to a turn-based match and automatch to fill any empty slots.
A protocol that handles when the status of turn-based matchmaking changes.
A participant in a turn-based match.

The participant joins the match and is an active player.
The participant declines the invitation to join the match, automatically terminating the match.
The participant leaves the match.
The participant is invited to the match, but hasn’t responded to the invitation.
The participant represents an unfilled position in the match that Game Center promises to fill when needed.
The participant is in an unexpected state.

No timeout to take a turn.
The abstract base protocol adopted by GameKit view controller classes.
A voice channel that allows players to speak with each other in a multiplayer game.

The state when the player connects to the channel.
The state when the player is connecting to the channel, but isn’t connected yet.

The [GKVoiceChatClient](/documentation/gamekit/gkvoicechatclient) protocol is implemented to control the behavior of the [GKVoiceChatService](/documentation/gamekit/gkvoicechatservice) object. The voice chat client has a number of responsibilities:
The [GKVoiceChatService](/documentation/gamekit/gkvoicechatservice) class allows your application to connect two iOS devices into a voice chat.
Error codes for the voice chat service error domain.
The audio hardware is unavailable to the voice chat service.
The voice chat service did not find an expected method defined by the client.
A serious error occurred inside the voice chat service.
The voice chat service didn’t recognize the call identifier.
A parameter had an unrecognized value.
A method on the voice chat service was called when it was not allowed to be called (for example, attempting to connect when the voice chat service was already connected).
The voice chat service had problems accessing the network.
The voice chat service stopped receiving packets from the remote participant.
The voice chat service was unable to allocate memory required to operate.
The remote participant is already connected to a voice chat.
A remote participant attempted to start a voice chat, then canceled.
A remote participant declined an invitation.
The remote participant in a voice chat stopped the chat.
Invalid data was received from a remote participant.
The voice chat service was unable to establish a connection with another user.
The application did not set a client before calling voice chat service methods.
The other participant is running a different version of the voice chat service.
An error occurred in [GKVoiceChatService](/documentation/gamekit/gkvoicechatservice).
Enable players to interact with friends, compare leaderboard ranks, earn achievements, and participate in multiplayer games.

t: Improving the player experience for games with large downloads
a: Provide ample content in your base installation and then use on-demand resources and the Background Assets API to handle additional content.
Enable Game Center, configure features, and test them locally in your Xcode project.

t: Letting players join matches using party codes
a: Add a rule that lets players invite players and join matches using a shared party code.
Game Center applies different type of rules you create in a particular order to find the best matches.

t: Protecting the player’s privacy using scoped identifiers
a: Use the scoped identifiers that GameKit provides you as player IDs when transmitting or saving player data.
Use achievements to motivate players and engage them more in your game.

t: Saving the player’s game data to an iCloud account
a: Save game data during play or after a game in the player’s iCloud account that’s accessible from any device.

t: Sending messages to players in turn-based games
a: Notify players of match events by sending messages and game data.

t: Starting turn-based matches and passing turns between players
a: Let Game Center store and forward match data between players in a turn-based game.
Test your matchmaking rules before you use them in your game.

t: Testing rule sets with player traffic using metrics
a: Get metrics on a new rule set with real match requests before releasing it.
